steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify the angle pair as corresponding angles
steps.content:
Lines $m$ and $n$ are parallel, crossed by transversal $t$. The angle of $170^{\circ}$ is formed at the intersection of $t$ and $m$, while angle $w$ is formed at the intersection of $t$ and $n$.

Both angles are positioned identically relative to their intersection points — in the same location with respect to the parallel lines and transversal.

These are corresponding angles.

steps.title: Apply the Corresponding Angles Postulate
steps.content: When a transversal intersects two parallel lines, corresponding angles are congruent. Since $170^{\circ}$ and $w$ are corresponding angles formed by transversal $t$ crossing parallel lines $m$ and $n$: $$w = 170^{\circ}$$ D is correct.
